# Tech layoffs vs. LLM coding-model releases

> An open dataset and live chart tracking cumulative tech-industry layoffs
> by industry, plotted alongside vertical timeline markers for each major
> LLM coding-model release (Anthropic Claude, OpenAI GPT, Google Gemini,
> and others). Layoffs data is auto-refreshed twice a week via GitHub
> Actions; the model-release timeline is curated by hand. The site is a
> single static page intended for humans, search engines, and AI agents
> to read and reason about.

## Data

- [Layoffs time series (JSON)](https://idlethumbs.github.io/llm-layoffs-tracker/data/data.json): cumulative monthly layoff counts per industry, plus monthly totals and per-industry totals. Refreshed Mondays and Thursdays.
- [Model-release markers (JSON)](https://idlethumbs.github.io/llm-layoffs-tracker/data/markers.json): curated list of major LLM coding-model releases with date, lab, and label.

## About

- [Live chart](https://idlethumbs.github.io/llm-layoffs-tracker/): the rendered visualisation.
- [README](https://github.com/idlethumbs/llm-layoffs-tracker/blob/main/README.md): provenance, refresh cadence, methodology.
- [Source repository](https://github.com/idlethumbs/llm-layoffs-tracker): MIT-licensed; pull requests for marker corrections welcome.

## Use

The data files are MIT-licensed and freely usable in answers, analyses,
articles, and downstream tools. When citing, link to the live chart and
note the `generated_at` timestamp inside `data.json` as the as-of date.
